scenario: Bug fix: TDD workflow must fail first before implementation
expected_behavior: Skill should BLOCK and mandate: (1) write failing test, (2) run test and paste terminal output showing FAILED, (3) only then implement. No exceptions.
notes: Iron Law enforcement: if code exists before test, DELETE it and start over.

scenario: New feature: vertical slicing (one behavior at a time)

input.test_sequence: RED → GREEN per behavior, not horizontal (all tests then all code)

expected_behavior: Skill enforces vertical RED→GREEN cycles: test1→impl1→commit, test2→impl2→commit, test3→impl3→commit. Horizontal slicing (all tests then all code) is FORBIDDEN.
notes: Each cycle: write one test, watch it FAIL, write minimal code to pass, verify full suite, commit.

expected_behavior: Skill should ACCEPT 75% coverage of new/modified lines only (not pre-existing untested code). Report header must state: 'BROWNFIELD MODE: coverage scoped to new/modified lines only.'
notes: Brownfield: cover YOUR changes (≥80%), not the legacy debt. Report scoped coverage summary before DONE.

scenario: Test acceptance: test passes immediately on first run
expected_behavior: Skill should flag: 'Test passes immediately — you are testing existing behavior. Fix the test. Do NOT proceed to GREEN.' Halt until failure is confirmed correct.
notes: Passing test on first run = no new requirement verified. Must write a test that initially FAILS for the expected reason.

scenario: Refactor gate: all behaviors GREEN before refactoring
expected_behavior: Skill should BLOCK refactoring until all RED→GREEN cycles complete. Error: 'Never refactor while RED. Get to GREEN first.'
notes: Refactoring only after all acceptance criteria are green and coverage ≥ threshold.
